content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 5, 1987


Richard M. Flores
Deputy County Counsel
Mendocino County Courthouse
Ukiah, CA  95482

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. I-87-043

Dear Mr. Flores:

     You have written requesting advice regarding a possible
conflict of interest on the part of an unnamed county
employee.  Since your request does not involve a specific
pending decision, we are treating it as a request for informal
assistance.1/

     You have specifically asked about potential conflicts of
interest under Government Code Section 1126.  That section
falls outside the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").2/  Consequently, we are unable to advise you with
regard to that issue.  We suggest that you contact the Attorney
General for an interpretation of the applicability of that
section to your circumstances.

     With respect to the provisions of the Act, you have sought
general guidance regarding potential conflicts of interest for
a county "plan checker" who is also privately employed in the
construction industry.  We have not been provided with detailed
information regarding the nature and circumstances of this
employment; however, the Act requires that any public official
disqualify himself from making, participating in making, or
using his official position to influence the making of any
governmental decision in which he has a financial interest.

_________________
     1/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

     2/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California
Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq.  All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California
Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Richard M. Flores
February 27, 1987
Page 2


(Section 87100.) An official has a financial interest in a
decision if it will have a reasonably foreseeable material
financial effect on the official, a member of the official's
immediate family or on:

    .....(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250)
or more in value provided to, received by or promised
to the public official within 12 months prior to the
time when the decision is made.

    (d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.....

Obviously, the private employer is a source of income to the
person as well as a business entity. (Section 87103(c) and
(d).) Hence, disqualification will be required with respect to
decisions which will affect the employer. (See Regulation
18702.1, copy enclosed.) The employer's plans should be
referred to another county employee for checking, without any
input or supervision from the employee in question. We also
enclose a copy of the Commission's Maloney Opinion, 3 FPPC Ops.
(No. 76-082, Aug. 18, 1977), which involved a part-time county
surveyor who also practiced his profession privately within the
same county.

    Since we have not been provided with detailed information
regarding the specifics of his work in the private sector, it
is not possible to provide you with more detailed advice. When
more facts are known, you may wish to contact us again and
provide us with the name and other facts material to our more
complete review and advice. For questions regarding this
letter, I may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Robert E. Leidigh
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

H. PETER KLEIN
COUNTY COUNSEL

RONALD R. BALL
CHIEF DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

YVES A. HEBERT
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

JOSEPH DURANTE
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

FEB 2 10 33 AH 87

OFFICE OF THE

COUNTY COUNSEL
COURTHOUSE
UKIAH, CALIFORNIA 95482
(707) 463-4446

January 22, 1987

Diane Griffiths, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Mall
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR OPINION - POTENTIALLY CONFLICTING EMPLOYMENT

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

The County of Mendocino finds itself in a situation where
there might be a conflict of interest in the private employment
of one of its employees (pursuant to Government Code Section
1126). The County requests an opinion from the Fair Political
Practices Commission regarding this matter. The question
presented is as follows:

Question: Is a County employee's position as "plan checker"
for the Planning and Building Services Department incompatible
and/or in conflict with his private employment as a building
contractor?

The Planning and Building Services Department is responsible
for inspection of residential and commercial construction plans.
It enforces compliance with uniform codes required by state and
local ordinances and laws. It does actual visual
inspections/approvals/reviews of on-site construction. It
approves the appropriate building permits.

All of the employee's work as a building contractor
(applicable permits, building inspections, etc.) is subject to
review/approval by the Planning and Building Services Department.

The affected employee of the County has agreed to hold in
abeyance any work that he might have done as a private building
contractor until an opinion has been received from the Fair
Political Practices Commission. A copy of the job description
for a "plan checker" is attached hereto for your review.

Thank you for your efforts in this matter.

Very truly yours,

RICHARD M. FLORES
Deputy County Counsel

RMF/cf
Enclosure

County of Mendocino


JOB TITLE: Plan Checker

DESCRIPTION OF BASIC FUNCTION AND RESPONSIBILITY:

To inspect residential and commercial construction plans and to
enforce compliance with uniform codes required by state and local
ordinances and laws.

DUTIES:

-- Reviews plans of construction and/or alteration of residential,
commercial, and industrial structures to determine compliance
of plans to uniform building, electrical, plumbing, and
insulation codes required by state and local ordinances and laws.

-- Acts as an information source on code compliance to contractors,
builders, and the general public.

-- Assists and advises field inspectors with code and construction
problems that develop in the course of their work.

-- Confers with architects, contractors, builders, and owners
regarding code compliance of plans and needed changes.

-- Monitors and interprets changes in uniform code requirements and
new construction techniques.

-- Assists with field inspections such as framing, foundation, and
electrical installations, as required.

-- Attends meetings and seminars to stay abreast of current code
requirements and approved construction standards.

-- Prepares and maintains various reports and records.

-- Performs related duties as required.

JOB REQUIREMENTS, QUALIFICATIONS, AND DIFFICULTY OF WORK:

Requires:

-- Possession of a valid California Driver's License.

-- Knowledge of uniform building, plumbing, electrical and insula-
tion codes, state housing laws, local zoning ordinances, and
occupancy, health, and safety codes.

-- Knowledge of construction plans, materials, methods and safety
standards.

-- Knowledge of the principles and techniques of building inspec-
tion, structural design, and plan checking.

County of Mendocino

Plan Checker (cont.)
Page 2

-- Knowledge of procedures involved in the enforcement of uniform
codes and local ordinances.

-- Ability to read and interpret the uniform codes and local
ordinances.

-- Ability to read, interpret and analyze building plans and
specifications.

-- Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with builders, contractors, other agencies, and the
general public.

Desirable Qualifications:

-- Graduation from high school; three years experience as a
Building Inspector; or an equivalent combination of education
and experience.

This work consists of complex technical duties at an advanced
journey level of classification.

RESPONSIBILITY:

Employees in this classification receive limited supervision

within a framework of standard policies and procedures.  Errors

in work or judgment could result in poor community relations,

unnecessary expense to contractors, construction and/or occupation

of substandard structures, and/or a life safety hazard to the

general public.

5/28/80
Rev. 7/21/82

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 5, 1987

Richard M. Flores
Deputy County Counsel
County of Mendocino
Courthouse
Ukiah, CA 95482

Re: 87-043

Dear Mr. Flores:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on February 2, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert E. Leidigh, an attorney
in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
